actions.binding_policy: docker_data.vhdx MUST NEVER be relocated onto F: (or any other USB/removable volume) while it remains the live Docker Desktop engine store. It stays on C: permanently. F: is approved only for cold offload / read-when-present governed assets with graceful degrade on disconnect (see policy rules 1-3 in qa/live_verification/f_drive_usb_policy_20260720.json).
actions.finding: F: is a Seagate BUP Slim external drive attached over USB (BusType=USB per Get-PhysicalDisk; confirmed again live this pass). The prior IN_PROGRESS plan relied on Get-Volume reporting `DriveType=Fixed`, which for an NTFS-formatted external HDD reflects media/filesystem removability, not the physical USB bus -- it is NOT proof of a permanent/non-hot-pluggable attachment. This SAME drive was found completely physically absent earlier the same day (qa/live_verification/docker_relocation_f_absent_blocked_20260720T1435Z.json), proving it genuinely disconnects mid-session. Hosting the live Docker Desktop data VHDX (docker_data.vhdx) on it would reproduce that exact daemon crash-loop / CVAT-outage failure mode on the next disconnect.

unblocks: Removes an active risk of a self-inflicted Docker/CVAT outage from a future F: USB disconnect during/after the relocation.

agent_executable_mitigation: The data/ junction already has a proven non-destructive C: fallback path (data_c_backup_relocated) that a sibling session used successfully when F: was absent; no new tooling needed there.
